text: Azure Activity Log recorded 3 administrative operation(s) by "admin@northwindsec.onmicrosoft.com" from IP 91.240.118.73 between 2026-05-16T03:12:44 and 2026-05-16T03:15:53 UTC. At 2026-05-16T03:12:44, a role assignment was created for a ServicePrincipal (role definition: b24988ac-6180-42a0-ab88-20f7382dd24c) at scope "/subscriptions/3f9d2a1e-7b4f-41d9-b2ea-9f51d67c1c83" (status: Succeeded). Granting roles to service principals at subscription scope is a privilege escalation technique (T1098.003 (Account Manipulation: Additional Cloud Roles)). At 2026-05-16T03:10:29, a new Azure AD application "AzureOpsAutomation" (appId: 9f3e91d3-f17a-4f0f-b4d0-8498fc5f89b1) was created. Registering applications can be used to create persistent OAuth tokens or service principals (T1136.003 (Create Account: Cloud Account)). At 2026-05-16T03:15:53, all secrets in Key Vault "prod-keyvault-01" were listed (status: Succeeded). Enumerating Key Vault secrets is a credential access technique that may expose API keys, passwords, and certificates. The combination of application creation and role assignment from an external IP is a common pattern in Azure tenant compromise, where attackers create persistent backdoor service principals with elevated permissions.
